summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of an outstanding 8/21/68 show highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, a killer St. Stephen → Eleven sequence and an all‑time great Alligator.
review: The performance is widely praised for its explosive second set – Dark Star, St. Stephen > Eleven, Death Don't Have No Mercy and a fiery Lovelight – with tight playing from Jerry, Phil and Pigpen. The soundboard source is clear and well‑mic'd (organ especially) though a few reviewers note a slightly flatter high end and some editing quirks. Overall the show is considered one of the peak “golden age” Dead concerts of 1968.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, praising both musical performance and recording quality; only one low rating cites editing issues. Weighted average and detailed commentary support a strong positive rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Well‑mic'd organ and clear mix, though some listeners note a slightly flatter sound with reduced high frequencies. Minor editing complaints (mis‑labelled "Drums" track) are mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set show; first set solid with Cryptical>Other One and Schoolgirl, second set a highlight of Live Dead material (Dark Star > St. Stephen > Eleven > Death Don't Have No Mercy) delivering the concert’s peak energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: On fire on Lovelight, strong guitar‑organ interplay with Phil on Dark Star, vocal low in Cryptical reprise, adds Goodnight theme near end.
band_member_comments.Phil: Weaves a psychic bass‑guitar dance with Jerry during Dark Star; solid foundation throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s memorable six‑note organ figure in Dark Star is frequently referenced; Pigpen’s Hammond organ work on Schoolgirl and Alligator receives high praise.
